one_line_takeaway: EvidenceRL turns evidence grounding into a GRPO training reward via sentence-level Focus-Then-Verify NLI scoring, substantially raising both accuracy and evidence-supported correctness over SFT/RAG/DPO baselines on medical and legal QA, while presenting empirical (not airtight) evidence against reward-proxy hacking via independent-verifier and untrained-baseline comparisons.
key_finding: On MIMIC cardiac diagnosis, EvidenceRL raises Llama-3.2-3B F1@3 from 37.0 to 54.5, Gmax@3 from 47.6 to 78.7 (Table 1; note the paper's own abstract/intro text says 78.2, but the Table 1 value is 78.7), EB from 31.8% to 61.6%, hallucinations from 11.6% to 2.4%, and Faithfulness from 72.5% to 87.5%. On BarExam MBE, Llama-3.1-8B Faithfulness rises from 32.8% to 67.6%, EB from 18.8% to 41.0%, hallucinations fall from 10.3% to 6.0%, and Gavg improves from -1.7 to 18.9. EvidenceRL narrows the scale-vs-baseline gap: its Llama-3.2-3B (F1@3=54.5) exceeds reasoning-only Llama-3.3-70B (51.3) and Gemma-3-27B (52.2). SFT collapses grounding while preserving accuracy (e.g., Gemma-3-27B Gavg@3 drops from 37.1 to 1.2 with F1@3 essentially unchanged at 36.6), showing demonstration tuning teaches citation format without semantic evidence use. fDPO raises Faithfulness (e.g., 82.2% on Llama-3.2-3B) mainly by shrinking the pool of correct-but-ungrounded predictions rather than by genuinely growing grounded-correct ones (EB moves only 31.8%->37.9%), unlike EvidenceRL's on-policy reward which grows EB directly (31.8%->61.6%) alongside accuracy. On the reward-hacking question specifically: the embedding-cosine correctness proxy showed 98.0% precision on GRPO-trained model outputs vs. an LLM judge (24,896 pairs, 145 false positives = 0.58%), statistically indistinguishable from the 97.2% precision on untrained reasoning-only outputs (38,629 pairs) -- the paper interprets this as no evidence that GRPO learned to exploit the correctness proxy. Cross-verifier check with an independent DeBERTa-v3-large NLI model confirmed grounding gains persisted across all 5 backbones (EB +1.0 to +16.0 pp, hallucinations -0.3 to -1.8 pp, Faithfulness +1.0 to +15.5 pp), though smaller in magnitude than under the training-time PubMedBERT NLI, attributed to DeBERTa-v3-large lacking biomedical pretraining.
core_contribution: EvidenceRL, a reinforcement-learning framework (GRPO) that makes evidence grounding a first-class, differentiable training objective rather than an inference-time filter. It combines three rewards: (1) a binary format reward for valid JSON output, (2) a semantic correctness reward using embedding-cosine similarity against references (with a threshold tau) for open-ended answers, or exact match for multiple-choice, and (3) a Focus-Then-Verify grounding reward that avoids context-overflow/signal-dilution by scoring sentence-level NLI entailment/contradiction between the model's reasoning and individual focused (anchor-context + evidence-section) premises, taking either the max-magnitude signed score (Gmax) or the average across premises (Gavg). The combined reward weights format=1, correctness=1, grounding=2, and is optimized on-policy with GRPO across group-normalized advantages. Evaluated on 5 backbones (Llama-3.2-3B, Llama-3.1-8B, Gemma-3-4B/12B/27B) against 8-backbone baselines (adding Llama-3.3-70B and GPT-oss-20B/120B) spanning Reasoning-Only, Self-RAG, Self-Consistency, SFT, and a Faithful-DPO (fDPO) preference baseline.
threat_model: Not an adversarial/attacker threat model. The paper's own relevant risk axis (Section 5.4, 'Reward Proxy Alignment Analysis') is reward hacking / proxy gaming in the RLHF-style sense: since the grounding reward comes from a frozen NLI verifier and the correctness reward from a cheap embedding-similarity proxy (not the more expensive LLM judge used only for evaluation), GRPO could in principle learn to exploit weaknesses in these proxy reward models rather than genuinely improving evidence use. The paper explicitly tests for this rather than assuming it away.
limitations: Grounding reward depends on a frozen biomedical NLI cross-encoder, which is only an approximation of true evidential reasoning (paper's own stated limitation), and the reward-hacking checks use precision/agreement with an LLM judge or a second NLI model rather than an exhaustive causal audit of policy behavior -- absence of detected proxy exploitation is not proof of its absence, just evidence within the tested comparison. Training cost is higher than SFT because GRPO requires sampling and scoring multiple completions per step. The method assumes retrieved/contextual evidence is itself reliable; errors in the knowledge source can propagate into 'well-grounded' but factually wrong outputs, since the grounding reward checks consistency with evidence, not the evidence's own correctness. The fDPO baseline used for comparison has a confound (cross-model preference pairs introduce implicit distillation from stronger to weaker backbones), which the paper flags as making a truly clean within-model grounding-only ablation an open follow-up. EvidenceRL was trained/evaluated on only 5 of the 8 backbones tested for baselines (no Llama-3.3-70B or GPT-oss-20B/120B GRPO runs are reported), so claims about the method 'narrowing the scale gap' rest on comparisons against larger models' zero-shot/baseline numbers, not on training EvidenceRL itself at those scales. Only two domains (cardiac diagnosis, legal MBE) were tested; the paper itself lists generalization to additional domains and evidence sources as future work. This is a research paper explicitly stated as not intended for autonomous clinical or legal deployment.

datasets_used: MIMIC-IV-Ext Cardiac (Cao and Zhao 2025; built on MIMIC-IV, Johnson et al. 2023): 3,700 train / 1,000 held-out ICU cases, ICD-10 'I'-prefix cardiac codes
BarExam MBE (Zheng et al. 2025): 954 train / 173 test cases across six legal subjects
quantitative_results: SFT ablation: Gemma-3-27B Gavg@3 collapses 37.1 -> 1.2 while F1@3 stays ~36.6 (Section 5.2)
fDPO on Llama-3.2-3B: Faithfulness 82.2%, F1@3 38.7 (vs. 37.0 baseline), EB only 31.8% -> 37.9% (Section 5.3)

Proxy-hacking check: embedding-cosine correctness proxy vs. LLM judge, tau=0.80: 98.0% precision on GRPO-trained outputs (24,896 pairs, 145 false positives), 97.2% precision on untrained reasoning-only outputs (38,629 pairs); recall capped at 58.0%, F1=73% (GRPO) vs F1=38% (reasoning-only) (Section 5.4, Figure 2)
Cross-verifier (DeBERTa-v3-large) re-evaluation of grounding gains across 5 backbones: EB +1.0 to +16.0 pp, H -0.3 to -1.8 pp, Faithfulness +1.0 to +15.5 pp (Section 5.4, Figure 3)

Ablation (Figure 4), F1@3 across Llama-3.2-3B/Gemma-3-4B/Gemma-3-12B: SFT 0.49/0.40/0.34; Reasoning-Only 0.37/0.44/0.46; GRPO(rc+rf) 0.575/0.497/0.542 (highest accuracy); GRPO(rc+rf+rg) 0.545/0.50/0.54 (best accuracy-grounding trade-off); corresponding Gavg@3: SFT 0.35/0.02/0.00, Reasoning-Only 0.45/0.34/0.22, GRPO(rc+rf) 0.564/0.394/0.258, GRPO(rc+rf+rg) 0.770/0.540/0.380
baselines_compared: Self-Consistency (Wang et al. 2022, N-sample majority voting)
SFT (supervised fine-tuning on curated diagnosis-reasoning pairs)
Faithful DPO / fDPO (cross-model preference pairs on NLI grounding quality, in the spirit of Context-DPO, Bi et al. 2025)

8 backbones tested for baselines: Gemma-3 (4B/12B/27B), Llama (3.2-3B/3.1-8B/3.3-70B), GPT-oss (20B/120B); EvidenceRL itself trained/reported on 5 of these (Llama-3.2-3B, Llama-3.1-8B, Gemma-3-4B/12B/27B)
